content.verdict: Homeric Poems is a tiny 10-suite boutique perched on the caldera in Firostefani, between Fira and Imerovigli. Every suite is a restored traditional Santorini cave dwelling — vaulted ceilings, whitewashed stone, hand-smoothed plaster, and a private terrace that faces directly into the sunset. The scale is the appeal: with just 10 keys, Homeric Poems feels like a private villa you share with a few other couples. The staff-to-guest ratio is extraordinary, and for a property of this size the polish is remarkable. Strictly adults-only, this is an excellent mid-luxury pick for honeymooners who want Perivolas-level intimacy without the Perivolas price tag.
content.best_room: Superior Cave Suite with outdoor jacuzzi and caldera view — a genuine cave dwelling on two levels, with a small private pool/hot tub on the terrace and unobstructed sunset view. The Honeymoon Cave Suite adds a larger terrace and a bigger plunge pool.

itinerary_7_nights.description: Airport transfer, welcome basket, sunset from your terrace with local wine.

itinerary_7_nights.description: Coffee on terrace, walk 15 minutes into Fira for breakfast, afternoon at the hotel's small infinity pool.

itinerary_7_nights.description: Private boat tour of the caldera, lunch on board at Thirasia.

itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning hike Firostefani to Imerovigli (20 min), lunch at Aegialos, nap, dinner at Selene.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Wine tour of Santo Wines, Venetsanos, and Estate Argyros with a driver
itinerary_7_nights.description: Wine tour of Santo Wines, Venetsanos, and Estate Argyros with a driver.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach day at Perissa or Vlychada, sunset dinner back in Firostefani
itinerary_7_nights.description: Beach day at Perissa or Vlychada, sunset dinner back in Firostefani.

caveats: Only 10 suites means booking is tight — reserve 5-6 months ahead for summer.
No full restaurant on site; breakfast is excellent, dinner requires walking into Fira or Firostefani village (5-15 minutes).
Some suites require climbing steps — mention any mobility needs when booking.
Firostefani is slightly less dramatic than Oia's cliff-edge but far less touristy.
The small pool is for cooling off, not serious swimming.

faqs.a: Yes, 16+. This is part of the intimate romantic atmosphere.

faqs.q: How is Firostefani compared to Oia?
faqs.a: Firostefani is quieter and more local, with the same caldera view. You'll walk or drive to Oia for the famous sunset crowds if you want them.

faqs.q: Does every room have a caldera view?
faqs.a: Yes, every suite faces the caldera — but the angle varies by unit. Upper-floor suites have the best view.

faqs.q: Is there a real restaurant on site?
faqs.a: No — only breakfast service and in-suite romantic dining by arrangement. Dinners are in nearby villages.
Perissa (black sand) is about 20 minutes by car; arrange a taxi or rental.

faqs.q: Is it worth the money vs Perivolas or Andronis?
faqs.a: If you want similar caldera-front intimacy at 40-50% less, yes. You'll trade a marquee restaurant and larger spa for genuine boutique charm.
